title: oxalis stictocheila
intro: Oxalis stictocheila, also known as the yellow wood sorrel, is a species of flowering plant in the wood sorrel family (Oxalidaceae) that is native to South America. The plant is rich in oxalic acid and has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and nausea, due to its antimicrobial and astringent properties. Oxalis stictocheila has various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, acting as an antioxidant, and supporting the immune system. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or tincture, and it can also be consumed as a raw leafy green, providing a rich source of vitamins and minerals.
